memoryArtifacts.headline: Seal of Stephen's wife, Elizabeth the Cuman

popularStory.headline: Élisabeth la Coumane (1240-1290)
popularStory.description:
Élisabeth la Coumane (en hongrois Kun Erzsébet, 1240 – entre 1290 et 1295) est une reine de Hongrie, épouse d'Étienne V de Hongrie et mère de Ladislas IV de Hongrie.

Biographie

Elle est la fille d'u …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné於1240年出生在匈牙利可馬洛-斯特格姆艾斯特格姆時，她的父親Хан Котен35歲，母親Agnez Riurikova de Halich21歲。她於1253年與Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király在匈牙利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1290年在匈牙利布達佩斯享年50歲，埋葬在德國海因里希_賀瑟-納斯奧可萊斯 賀爾沙福特 史馬爾卡爾登澤利根塔爾。

nameMeanings.name: Kun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Khaim, Rozalia, Sarra.  Hungarian Istvan, Laszlo, Balazs, Zoltan.  Russian Boris, Betya, Liliya, Milya, Vladimir, Yefim, Yelizaveta, Zoya.

Hungarian: from kún, an ethnic name for a member of a Turkic people known in English as the Cumanians. The Cumanians settled in eastern Hungary after the Mongol invasion in the 13th century; several villages (compare Kuni 2) and a whole country of Hungary is named after them. Compare Cun .
Jewish (from Hungary): adoption of the name in 1 above in place of the original German-based homophonic surname   Kuhn  .

variants.name: Kuni
meanings: German: from a pet form of the personal name Kuno, Kunrat (see Konrad ). Compare Kuney .
Hungarian: habitational name for someone from a place such as Kun, named after the Turkic Cumanian people (see   Kun   1).

Japanese: written 国 ‘country’ or ‘kingdom’ and sometimes also 邦; both are rare today. It is written phonetically 六合 in Amami Island. Found in the Shinsen shōjiroku. Some instances in America may be shortened versions of longer names.

variants.name: Cun
meanings: Amerindian (Guatemala; also Cún): Mayan name, from a Castilianized form of k’un ‘squash’.

nameMeanings.name: Erzsébet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: The Mongolian Tatars devastated the country. Their presence, which lasted a year, halted development for at least a century. After the warfare with the Hungarians, the Tatars did not continue towards the west.

en.lifeSummary: When Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné was born in 1240, in Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Hungary, her father, Хан Котен, was 35 and her mother, Agnez Riurikova de Halich, was 21. She married Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király in 1253, in Hungary. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1290, in Budapest, Hungary, at the age of 50, and was buried in Seligenthal, Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hesse-Nassau, Prussia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné est née en 1240, à Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Hongrie, son père, Хан Котен, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Agnez Riurikova de Halich, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király en 1253, à Hongrie. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1290, à Budapest, Hongrie, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Seligenthal, Kreis Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hessen-Nassau, Prusse, Allemagne.

de.Seligenthal: Seligenthal, Kreis Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hessen-Nassau, Preußen, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné 1240 in Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Ungarn geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Хан Котен 35 und ihre Mutter Agnez Riurikova de Halich 21. Sie hat 1253 in Ungarn Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1290 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Budapest, Ungarn und wurde in Seligenthal, Kreis Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hessen-Nassau, Preußen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné è nata il nel 1240 nel Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Ungheria, sua padre, Хан Котен, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Agnez Riurikova de Halich, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király nel 1253, nel Ungheria. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1290, nel Budapest, Ungheria, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Seligenthal, Kreis Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hessen-Nassau, Preußen, Germania.

ko.Seligenthal: 독일 프레우ss 헤센-나소 크레이스 허르샤프트 슈마캘든 셀익솔
ko.lifeSummary: Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné(이)가 1240년 헝가리 코a롬-스즈터고 스즈터고에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Хан Котен(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnez Riurikova de Halich(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1253년 헝가리에서 Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1290년 헝가리 버다페스트에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 프레우ss 헤센-나소 크레이스 허르샤프트 슈마캘든 셀익솔에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné nasceu em 1240, em Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Hungria, seu pai, Хан Котен, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Agnez Riurikova de Halich, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király em 1253, em Hungria. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. ru.Seligenthal: селиджентэл, крис хиррщафт щмалкалден, хессен-нассау, Пруссия, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné родилась в 1240 г. в местности эсзтиргом, комaром-есзтиргом, Венгрия, её отцу по имени Хан Котен было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnez Riurikova de Halich, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király в 1253 г. в местности Венгрия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1290 г. в местности будапест, Венгрия в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности селиджентэл, крис хиррщафт щмалкалден, хессен-нассау, Пруссия, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Kun Erzsébet Magyar Királyné nació en 1240, en Esztergom, Komárom-Esztergom, Hungría, su papá, Хан Котен, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Agnez Riurikova de Halich, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Árpád-házi V. István Magyar Király en 1253, en Hungría. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1290, en Budapest, Hungría, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Seligenthal, Kreis Herrschaft Schmalkalden, Hessen-Nassau, Preußen, Alemania.
